copy.title: Late-Clock Corner Backdoor — Left Side
copy.prompt: Left corner is denied. 7 on the clock. What now?
copy.game_context: 4-on-4 half-court. Ball at the top of the key, 7 on the shot clock. Defense is denying the swing to the left corner.
copy.possession_setup: The ball is at the top of the key. You are in the left corner. x2 is denying the swing — his hand is in the lane between you and the ball, hips closed off from the rim, low-man (x4) hovering at the nail.
copy.decision_moment: x2 has his hand in your passing lane and his hips closed off from the basket. The corner pass is dead. Clock is at 7 — somebody has to make the read now.
copy.visible_cue: x2's hand is in your lane, hips turned toward the sideline, eyes on the ball. x4 is a step off the nail but not yet rotated up.
copy.best_read: Rip your outside foot baseline and back-cut hard behind x2 to the front of the rim. The passer leads you to the layup before x4 rotates up; the shot clock buys the window the swing pass would have wasted.
copy.explanation_md: **Late-Clock Backdoor — other side.** A corner denial works the same on the left as the right. x2's hand is in the lane and his hips are closed off from the rim — he's guarding the swing pass, not the basket. With 7 on the clock the defense knows the swing is coming and pre-commits to it. The only catch they can't recover to is the one behind them. **Rip baseline. Plant. Cut hard. Show your hands at the rim.**

feedback.correct: Right read. Same corner denial, left side. You beat x4's rotation before the clock decided.
feedback.partial: V-cut keeps the play alive, but you spent half the clock to do it. The corner denial was the layup window.
feedback.wrong: Read his body, not your spot. Hand in the lane = the rim is open behind him.

self_review_checklist: Did I see x2's hand cutting off the corner pass?
Did I rip the outside foot baseline before I committed?
Did I cut hard, like I wanted the layup?
Did I beat x4's rotation up from the nail?
acceptable_reads: V-cut up to the wing relief — keeps the play alive, but the clock decides on the next bounce.
bad_reads: Stand in the corner and call for it — x2's hand deflects the entry.
Front-cut into the paint — x4 steps up and the cut dies at the rim.

back_cut_baseline_to_rim.label: Rip baseline and back-cut behind him to the rim.
back_cut_baseline_to_rim.feedback_text: Best read. Hand in the lane means the rim is open. You beat x4 to the front of the basket before the clock could decide.

v_cut_to_relief.label: V-cut up to the wing for a relief catch.
v_cut_to_relief.feedback_text: Okay. The relief catch keeps the play alive, but the corner denial was the layup window you just spent the clock on.
v_cut_to_relief.partial_feedback_text: Relief keeps the possession — and trades the rim for the clock. Back-cut next time.

stand_in_corner.label: Stand in the corner and call for it.
stand_in_corner.feedback_text: Not yet. His hand is already in the lane. Standing still hands him the deflection.

front_cut_to_paint.label: Front-cut up into the paint.
front_cut_to_paint.feedback_text: Not yet. Front cuts let him stay attached and x4 steps up — the cut dies before it reaches the rim.
